biodiversity_threats.description: Temperatures exceeded the heatwave threshold of 30.22°C, putting coral reefs and stationary marine life at high risk of thermal stress and bleaching.
Extreme nutrient pollution detected in 34 zones. This triggers explosive algal growth that depletes ocean oxygen, leading to mass suffocation of fish, crabs, and bottom-dwellers.

A severe drop in foundational phytoplankton detected in 17117 zones. This disrupts the exact base of the marine food web, forcing massive commercial fish populations, dolphins, and whales to migrate elsewhere to survive.
